title: crotalaria polysperma subsp. stewartii
intro: Crotalaria polysperma subsp. stewartii, also known as the Stewart's rattle pod, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, it is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and treating skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Crotalaria polysperma subsp. stewartii are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its therapeutic properties.
